title: carya × ludoviciana
intro: Carya × ludoviciana, also known as the sweet pignut or swamp hickory, is a hybrid plant of the hickory genus, believed to be a cross between Carya alba and Carya texana. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its bark, leaves, and roots, which contain various compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The health benefits of Carya × ludoviciana include its potential to reduce inflammation, act as an antioxidant, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Carya × ludoviciana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
